Name: Label
Description: Use the label above the title set the context for the information in the dialog.
Content Rules: The title should be brief and clearly describe the dialogs’s task or purpose.

Name: Task title
Description: The header describes the dialogs’s task or purpose.
Content Rules: The title should be brief and clearly describe the dialogs’s task or purpose.

Name: Content area
Description: Contains the information and/or controls needed to complete the modal’s task. It can include message text and components.
Dynamic Behavior: When the content exceed the maximum allowed vertical space, the scrollbar appears and is limited the content area only.
Error State: We should wait to validate any and all inputs when the CTA is clicked and not before.

Name: Progress bar
Description:
Shows which step the current task is on.

The progress bar determine the percentage of the completed task.

It contains a background and stepper bars. There will always be more than one stepper bars.
Dynamic Behavior:
On moving to the next step, the stepper in the progress bar changes from  Grey30 to Blue60.

The height of the progress bar is 4 px

Based on the number of steps in wizard , the progress bar will have steppers. The space between the steppers is 4 px.
Default State:
The background is always Grey20. 

The stepper has two states. First stepper will always be Blue60 and the rest will be Grey30.

Name: CTA bar
Description: There are three buttons in the Wizard CTA bar: Previous, Cancel, Continue.
Dynamic Behavior:
In the first step, the CTA bar includes Cancel and Continue buttons
In the middle steps, the CTA bar includes the Previous, Cancel, and Continue buttons.
In the last step, the CTA bar includes Previous, Cancel and complete task buttons

Name: Cancel button
Description: Clicking the Cancel button will close the modal and return the user to its previous context. Cancel undoes all applied changes.

Name: Previous button
Dynamic Behavior: Save users edit content for them to re-edit or review later

Name: Primary CTA button
Description: Clicking the primary action will guide users to the next step to complete multiple tasks.
Dynamic Behavior:
The primary CTA would be Continue guiding users to the next step.
In the last step of the sequence, the Continue button label should change to reflect the final action.
The primary CTA should NEVER be disabled.
